event.summary: Reviewed the bounded proposal and made it claimable without expanding its repositories, paths, or prerequisites.

acceptance.proof: One caller-selected provider call returns all four runtime-owned values with one witness; no mixed-provider composition or ambient acquisition occurs.
Provider identity, epoch, and revision evidence is separately inspectable and must exactly match the caller's expected owner witness; no clock or arrival-time freshness is inferred.
CreatedAt, Uid, SessionId, then Hostname are checked as opaque Unicode-scalar text against the existing O runtime maximum with deterministic first-failure evidence and preserved accepted allocations.
Accepted state consumes into exactly four correctly labelled S provider values and neither constructs nor admits a complete volatile snapshot.
Feature/dependency closure remains empty and no clock, environment, host, transport, socket, network, device, activation, or Manifold authority surface opens.

document.commit_policy: Commit the four-value runtime acquisition/evidence lane separately from platform acquisition, T, transport providers, complete S admission, activation, networking, devices, and authority.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the four-value runtime provider, shared witness, S separation, and ambient-acquisition prohibition.
Describe the explicit runtime-lane adapter without claiming platform acquisition or activation.
Review shared owner-witness guidance; retain unchanged because artifact and authority separation already cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review installed skill without public mutation.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review repo routing; retain unchanged because LSL and Manifold boundaries do not change.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review installed skill without public mutation.

non_scope: Modification of accepted O, S, or T source/evidence; Cargo manifests/locks; project/feature locks; dependencies; features; unsafe; native libraries; oracle drivers; external implementation inputs.

Clock reads, time generation or parsing, identity generation or uniqueness, environment variables, hostname/OS/filesystem inspection, randomness, process spawning, provider discovery/selection, retry/background work, or platform adapters.

Implementation or transport lane acquisition, complete LSLC-001S snapshot construction/admission, XML/document behavior, sockets, networking, protocol, wire, discovery, inlet, outlet, buffering, recovery, FFI, runtime activation, devices, packages, or permissions.
Manifold admission, identity, registry, subscription, route, lease, revision, replay, authorization, audit, session, topology, transport, provider epoch, command, or product authority.
document.objective: Add one dependency-free explicitly invoked adapter contract that acquires created_at, uid, session_id, and hostname together from one caller-selected runtime provider, retains one separately inspectable shared owner identity/epoch/revision witness, requires exact expected-witness match, validates the four opaque values in LSLC-001O order, and projects exactly the LSLC-001S runtime lane without complete snapshot admission or ambient platform acquisition.
outputs: One synchronous caller-selected provider trait returning four opaque runtime values and one shared owner witness in one call.
Bounded owner identity plus exact owner-issued epoch/revision evidence retained separately from the four values.
Typed provider, witness, and fixed-order value-bound failures with accepted allocation preservation.
A consuming projection to exactly CreatedAt, Uid, SessionId, and Hostname LSLC-001S values without invoking complete admission.
